comment.base: Look, people, I understand that people get emotional about these issues. But standing in the way of law enforcement, impeding justice, and interfering with officers does nothing to solve the problem. It solves absolutely nothing. If you want change, lawmakers are the ones who need to be approached. Your representatives at the local, state, and national level are the ones who should be held accountable. You elected them to represent you and to create, modify, amend, or eliminate laws. That is where pressure belongs. Showing up at the point of enforcement and interfering with officers who are enforcing laws that already exist is not activism. Doing that based on emotion, belief systems, or personal ideology is not effective. Activism is changing the actual laws that law enforcement is required to enforce. If people want to protest effectively, they should be protesting outside of Congress, state capitols, and the offices of lawmakers who write the laws. Not at the point of enforcement. What is happening right now is people crying over the effects instead of addressing the causes. People need to wake up and be smart. Be effective. Stop being emotional. Highly emotional people act irrationally, and that is exactly where this behavior is leading us. You also seem to forget that these officers are licensed. Many ICE and Customs officers are licensed law enforcement officers. They are federal law enforcement officers with arrest authority and all the legal powers granted to peace officers of this country. Calling them “just ICE” does not change that reality. They deserve respect. They have families. They have a job to do. They are operating within the bounds of the law, even if you feel they are not. Your feelings do not override the law. Harassing officers, impeding operations, obstructing justice, and showing up armed or creating chaos does not help anyone. It only puts officers and civilians at risk and puts you in legal jeopardy. If you want real change, be intelligent in your messaging. Be strategic. Change the laws. Do not interfere with law enforcement doing the job the law requires them to do.

comment.base: Here’s what we do know. ICE agents were conducting a targeted federal enforcement operation in Minneapolis, Minnesota. This was not random. This was not a sweep of innocent bystanders. It was a lawful operation carried out by trained federal agents in a major U.S. city. The individual they were attempting to detain was JOSE HUERTA-CHUMA, an illegal alien from Ecuador with a documented criminal history, including domestic assault, attempted bodily harm, and disorderly conduct. That information was released by DHS. These are facts. During that operation, ALEX PRETTI, a 37-year-old U.S. citizen and nurse, inserted himself into the situation. This is the part the left refuses to be honest about. Pretti was not peacefully protesting from the sidelines. He was not merely observing. He physically interfered with an active federal enforcement operation. He chose to place himself between ICE agents and the individual they were lawfully attempting to detain, and he did so while armed, carrying a handgun and additional magazines. Official narratives are contradicted by video evidence, and still no one is disciplined, charged, or even clearly identified. I do not accept that this is “complicated.” I do not accept that agencies should be allowed to investigate themselves while the public is expected to trust the outcome. When armed federal agents operate with secrecy, anonymity, and immunity from consequences, that is not law enforcement — it is unaccountable state power. This is not about politics. This is about basic governance and public safety. If this continues without accountability, then the failure is not only on the agencies — it is on Congress for allowing it. The public is watching.
